---
pagetitle: "eMANDEVAL Future | Mesfin Genie"
description: "A decision-support tool for comparing vaccine-mandate designs by predicted public support, expected health outcomes, and benefit-cost trade-offs."
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell">
<p class="eyebrow">Tool · Vaccine policy</p>
<h1>eMANDEVAL Future</h1>
<p>Prospective analysis of vaccine-mandate policy designs.</p>
</div></header>

<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow">
<h2>The problem</h2>
<p>Vaccine mandates are bundles of design choices, and public support, health benefits, and costs move together. Comparing designs "by eye" is difficult. eMANDEVAL lets users explore how design choices change predicted support and outcomes.</p>
<h2>Intended users</h2>
<p>Public-health policymakers, researchers, and partner agencies considering mandate designs.</p>
<h2>Inputs and outputs</h2>
<p>Users select mandate design features; the tool returns predicted public support, expected health outcomes, and benefit-cost comparisons across alternative designs.</p>
<h2>Methodological basis</h2>
<p>Predictions draw on preference estimates from discrete choice experiments on vaccine-mandate designs, developed within the MandEval programme of work.</p>
<h2>Developer</h2>
<p>Developed by Dr Mesfin Genie, Newcastle Business School, the University of Newcastle.</p>
<h2>Limitations</h2>
<p>Estimates are based on stated preferences and modelling assumptions and are intended to inform, not to determine, policy decisions.</p>
<p><a class="button-primary" href="/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Open the tool</a></p>
</div></section>
<script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"SoftwareApplication","name":"eMANDEVAL Future","applicationCategory":"Decision support tool","operatingSystem":"Web","url":"https://mesfingenie.com/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Mesfin Genie"}}</script>

```
